title: RBAC: Create Role and RoleBinding
description:
## Restrict Namespace Access with RBAC

A CI/CD service account needs read-only access to pods and deployments in the `staging` namespace.

**Your tasks:**
1. Create namespace `staging`
2. Create a **ServiceAccount** named `ci-reader` in the `staging` namespace
3. Create a **Role** named `read-workloads` in `staging` that allows `get`, `list`, `watch` on `pods` and `deployments`
4. Create a **RoleBinding** named `ci-reader-binding` that binds `read-workloads` to the `ci-reader` ServiceAccount
5. Verify the ServiceAccount **can** list pods but **cannot** create them

hints.title: Create namespace and service account
hints.body: Create these first before the role, as the RoleBinding references both.
hints.command:
kubectl create namespace staging
kubectl create serviceaccount ci-reader -n staging

hints.title: Create the Role
hints.body: Use `kubectl create role` with multiple `--verb` and `--resource` flags.

hints.title: Bind the Role
hints.body: RoleBinding ties a Role to a subject. ServiceAccount subjects need namespace-qualified names.

hints.title: Verify permissions with auth can-i
hints.body: Use `--as` to impersonate the service account and test its permissions.
hints.command:
kubectl auth can-i list pods --as=system:serviceaccount:staging:ci-reader -n staging
kubectl auth can-i create pods --as=system:serviceaccount:staging:ci-reader -n staging

validation.description: Validates the full RBAC chain: namespace, SA, role, rolebinding, and effective permissions.

commands.command: kubectl get serviceaccount ci-reader -n staging -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
kubectl get role read-workloads -n staging -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
kubectl get rolebinding ci-reader-binding -n staging -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
kubectl auth can-i list pods --as=system:serviceaccount:staging:ci-reader -n staging
kubectl auth can-i create pods --as=system:serviceaccount:staging:ci-reader -n staging
